content.thinking: Let me find the latest screenshot in the Screenshots folder.

input.command: ls "C:/Users/Administrator/Pictures/Screenshots/" 2>/dev/null || echo "ํด๋ ์์ ๋๋ ๋น์ด์์"

input.command: powershell -Command "Get-ChildItem 'C:\Users\Administrator' -Recurse -Include '*.png','*.jpg','*.jpeg','*.bmp' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { \$_.LastWriteTime -gt (Get-Date).AddDays(-7) } | Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 10 FullName, LastWriteTime"
